meaning: adj.<美俚>蹩脚的,低劣的,丑的,脏的,邋遢的
examples: A new lycée will open in Kentish Town (near grungy Camden Town) this year.
His USES of grungy textured backgrounds and typography are unmistakable.
The locals are eating lunch in makeshift kitchens down grungy back alleys.
In 1998 he hired Marc Jacobs, an American designer mainly known for his grungy collections.
These items were so grungy-looking it was an insult to think that they might belong to her mother.
